subject: How to remove the Corvid's Raid token once it's face up

posts.body:
I was playing Crows and flipped a raid token face up to get some much needed points.

Fool!

Suddenly I was without one of the handiest plot tokens and everyone was giving it a wide berth. 

Is there any way for it to be removed from the board other than for an enemy to attack it? 

Google told me I could attack it myself but the Law of Root states that battle is against enemy pieces, so I don't agree…
You can only attack enemy pieces, as you say, and you're not an enemy of yourself. And yes, I think the only way to remove it is that enemies should attack it.
Because other factions aren’t incentivised to attack and remove raid tokens, leverage that to help you score points by flipping other tokens instead.

Don't forget you can also use the Trick action to move that plot somewhere else. (Provided you have a different revealed plot on the board during your turn.) Perhaps enemies would be more incentivized to remove it if it was somewhere else?

But I agree with the others — a Raid plot usually does more for you when it's revealed and boosting your scoring for the other revealed plots than it does when it's removed.

pmw57 wrote:

Because other factions aren’t incentivised to attack and remove raid tokens, leverage that to help you score points by flipping other tokens instead.

In response to the OP, I am not sure it is the handiest token. I wouldn't differentiate but if forced to choose it would be the one that gives you more cards (exploit?).

But Paul hits the nail on the head.....if you have 2 Raid tokens that are flipped and your opponents will not attack, that means every subsequent flipped token is scoring at least 3 points........they'll attack it soon enough
